name: Irish Pot-Roasted Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. If there are giblets with the bird, remove them, wash all but the liver (reserve for another use), and cover in water. Add salt and pepper, bring to a boil, and simmer for half an hour.
2. Wipe the chicken inside and out, removing any lumps of fat. Sprinkle with salt.
3. Mix together the oatmeal, chopped onion, butter or suet, stock, and seasoning. Stuff the chicken with this mixture and secure well.
4. Heat the dripping or oil and lightly fry the bacon. Chop and add to a casserole.
5. Quickly brown the chicken in the same fat and place on top of the bacon.
6. Soften the onions and briefly sauté the carrots, then add them to the casserole.
7. Strain the giblet stock and bring it up to about 1/2 liter. Heat and pour over the chicken.
8. Cover and cook in a moderate oven (350°C) for about an hour.
9. Meanwhile, cut the potatoes into thick slices and blanch them in boiling water or steam for about 5 minutes. Toss in seasoned flour and add to the casserole, adding a little more giblet stock if needed.
10. Cover with buttered wax paper and continue cooking for another 1/2 hour, removing the paper for the last few minutes for browning.
